summary:

Altana’s report also highlights reliance on Taiwanese semiconductor manuf

summary:

title: Isaacman expects Chinese crewed mission around the moon in 2027
summary:
Isaacman expects Chinese crewed mission around the moon in 2027 
The head of NASA says he expects China to perform a crewed flight around the moon in

title: SDA director Sandhoo takes on broader Space Force missile warning portfolio
summary: The Pentagon is realigning the Space Development Agency’s satellite programs under a new acquisition structure. SDA director Sandhoo is taking on a broader role in the Space Force's missile warning portfolio.

title: Startup challenging satellite industry’s multi-orbit playbook
summary: Contrivian, a startup, challenges the satellite industry's traditional multi-orbit approach, arguing that LEO constellations alone can offer better resilience and speed. The company claims that combining GEO, MEO, and LEO satellites introduces networking complications.

title: Portal Space taps Quindar for ground mission support of its maneuvering spacecraft
summary: Portal Space has selected Quindar to provide ground mission support for its upcoming maneuverable spacecraft, which targets the military market. This collaboration aims to enhance the operational capabilities of Portal Space's spacecraft.

title: Vast announces line of high-power satellite buses
summary: Vast, a commercial space station developer, is expanding into satellite manufacturing with a new line of high-power satellite buses.

title: Lynk and Anterix get FCC nod to test satellite D2D for private utility networks
summary: Lynk Global received FCC approval to test direct-to-device satellite services for private utility networks using Anterix's terrestrial spectrum in the U.S. The collaboration aims to enhance connectivity for utility infrastructure.

title: Anderson confirmed as NASA deputy administrator
summary: The U.S. Senate confirmed Matt Anderson as NASA's deputy administrator on May 18, making him the second-in-command of the space agency. This appointment positions him as a key leader within NASA.

title: An astronaut's view of Argentina's snow-capped mountains | Space photo of the day for May 20, 2026
summary: An astronaut captured a stunning space photo of Argentina's snow-capped mountains. The image highlights the beauty of Earth from space.

title: NASA's Psyche probe takes awesome images of Mars on way to (possibly) precious asteroid
summary: NASA's Psyche spacecraft performed a gravity assist flyby of Mars, capturing close-up images during its journey to a potentially valuable asteroid. The mission aims to study the metal-rich asteroid Psyche, which could offer insights into planetary cores.

title: How to watch SpaceX launch its 1st Starship V3 megarocket on May 21
summary: SpaceX is set to launch its first Starship V3 megarocket on May 21, with live coverage available for the highly anticipated event.

title: SpaceX launches Starlink satellites on stunning sunset Falcon 9 flight (video)
summary: SpaceX launched 24 Starlink satellites aboard a Falcon 9 rocket from Vandenberg Space Force Base in California on May 19, 2026. The mission featured a visually striking sunset liftoff.

title: Lasers in moon craters could create a lunar GPS system
summary: Scientists are exploring using lasers in the moon's coldest craters to create a GPS-like navigation system for future lunar missions. This technology could enhance precision for Artemis astronauts and spacecraft navigating the lunar surface.

title: Worker dies at SpaceX's Starbase ahead of Starship V3 megarocket launch
summary: A worker died at SpaceX's Starbase facility in South Texas ahead of the planned debut launch of the Starship V3 megarocket. The incident occurred as the company prepared for the highly anticipated flight.

title: 'The Mandalorian and Grogu' clip reveals how Mando gets his Razor Crest spaceship back (video)
summary: A clip from 'The Mandalorian and Grogu' reveals how Din Djarin retrieves his Razor Crest spaceship. The scene hints at future adventures with a new mission.

title: Vast, builder of private space stations, launches line of high-power satellites
summary: Vast, a California-based startup focused on private space stations, has announced the launch of a new line of high-power satellites. This move marks an expansion beyond their original space station plans.

title: See Jupiter glow beside the waxing moon after sunset on May 20
summary: On May 20, Jupiter will be visible near the waxing moon after sunset. This celestial event offers a chance to observe the gas giant alongside Earth's moon.

title: NASA’s Fermi Glimpses Power Source of Supercharged Supernovae
summary: NASA's Fermi Gamma-ray Space Telescope detected a rare, luminous supernova powered by a supermagnetized neutron star. The findings suggest the neutron star's energy boosted the supernova's brightness.

title: Fire Chars Santa Rosa Island
summary: A fire has burned across the southern side of Santa Rosa Island, the second-largest island in California's Channel Islands National Park. The blaze highlights ongoing wildfire risks in protected natural areas.

title: NASA’s Psyche Mission Images Mars’ Huygens Crater
summary: NASA's Psyche mission captured an enhanced-color image of Mars' Huygens Crater using its multispectral imager. The crater, about 470 kilometers in diameter, is located in the heavily cratered southern highlands.

title: NASA’s Psyche Mission Spies Mars’ Wind-Blown Craters During Close Approach
summary: NASA's Psyche spacecraft captured images of Mars' wind-blown craters during a close approach on May 15, 2026. The images show streaks formed by wind blowing over impact craters in the Syrtis Major region.

title: Psyche’s High-Resolution View of Mars’ South Pole
summary: NASA's Psyche mission captured the highest-resolution view of Mars' south polar cap during a gravity assist flyby. The image shows water ice-rich regions with a resolution of about 0.7 miles per pixel.

title: NASA’s Psyche Mission Sees Mars’ South Pole After Flyby
summary: NASA's Psyche spacecraft captured its first view of a nearly full Mars during a flyby on May 15, 2026, showcasing the south polar cap and Valles Marineris canyon system. The mission will now continue its journey after the planetary encounter.

title: NASA’s Psyche Mission Images the Crescent of Mars
summary: NASA's Psyche mission captured a crescent image of Mars during a gravity assist flyby on May 15, 2026. This was the last full-planet view before the spacecraft's field of view became overfilled.

title: NASA’s Psyche Mission Aces Mars Flyby, Targets Metal-Rich Asteroid
summary: NASA's Psyche spacecraft successfully completed a close flyby of Mars on May 15, using a gravity assist to adjust its trajectory toward a metal-rich asteroid. The maneuver provided a critical speed boost without consuming onboard propellant.

title: Moon-Venus Conjunction
summary: The Moon and Venus appeared in conjunction above the Washington Monument in Washington on May 18, 2026, as viewed from NASA Headquarters. This celestial alignment is an optical illusion, as the two celestial bodies are actually millions of miles apart.

title: Johnson’s Cindy Evans Prepares Artemis Teams for Lunar Science
summary: NASA's Artemis II crew served as scientific ambassadors during their lunar mission, analyzing and photographing geologic features like impact craters. Cindy Evans from Johnson Space Center prepared the teams for these scientific responsibilities.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Electron, and Vega C launch missions alongside Starship Flight 12
summary: Six space launches are scheduled globally in the next week, including SpaceX's 12th Starship flight, a Falcon 9 mission, an Electron launch, and a Vega C mission.

title: Vega C launches SMILE to study Earth’s magnetosphere
summary: ESA's SMILE mission launched aboard Vega C to study Earth's magnetosphere. The mission aims to investigate the interaction between solar wind and Earth's magnetic field.

title: Super Heavy Block 3 the Booster of the Future
summary: The article discusses the evolution of the Super Heavy Block 3 booster, the first stage of SpaceX's Starship. It highlights the ongoing changes and advancements being made to this critical component of future space missions.

title: Artemis III deep into processing and planning for key HLS test
summary: NASA is advancing planning for Artemis III, a critical crewed mission to the Moon. The mission includes key Human Landing System (HLS) testing.

title: Flight 12 readies for the debut SpaceX’s next Ship evolution
summary: SpaceX is preparing for Flight 12 of its Starship second stage, which has undergone multiple iterations since the early test flights of SN8. The upcoming launch marks another step in the evolution of the Starship program.

title: Rocket Lab signs new launch contracts and acquires robotics company during Q1 2026
summary: Rocket Lab achieved record-breaking performance in Q1 2026 by signing new launch contracts and acquiring a robotics company. The company surpassed all its 2025 milestones during this period.

title: SpaceX’s new Starship pad readies for first launch
summary: SpaceX has completed construction of a redesigned launch pad for its Starship rocket. The new pad is now preparing for its first launch.

title: SpaceX CRS-34 launches to ISS on third attempt
summary: SpaceX successfully launched the CRS-34 mission to the International Space Station (ISS) on its third attempt, following a previous docking of Progress MS-34. The mission marks another resupply effort to support the ISS.

title: Launch Preview: SpaceX and Chinese missions fill busy launch manifest
summary: SpaceX and Chinese missions are set to conduct nine launches in the week of May 11. The busy launch manifest highlights high activity in both the United States and China.

title: Full Stack Flight 12 completes WDR
summary: Full Stack Flight 12 completes Wet Dress Rehearsal (WDR) after Booster 19's successful static fire test. The event marks a key milestone for the partner involved.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry news item, but the specific details were not provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication marks five decades of the European Space Agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Researchers in ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section, including many young contributors, test and develop materials to ensure spacecraft durability in space. Their work supports the success of ESA's missions.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) to enhance space exploration through training, operations, simulations, and public engagement. This initiative aims to improve efficiency and accessibility in space missions.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical test for spacecraft parachutes, ensuring they can withstand high temperatures during re-entry. This bake-out process is essential for the safety of future missions.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA's ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-meter parachute for Mars by sterilizing it to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone to prevent microbial contamination. The parachute, made of nylon and Kevlar, will slow the rover during its landing on Mars, launching in 2028 to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing objects in space. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, remaining on track for a 2027 launch to search for terrestrial planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA will hold its annual SME Forum in June 2026 to gather feedback from small and medium-sized enterprises in the space sector. The event includes an online preparatory forum in April and May, followed by in-person sessions at ESA Headquarters in Paris.

title: Dems Warn Golden Dome Will Accelerate the Nuclear Arms Race
summary: Democratic lawmakers argue that the Golden Dome missile defense system could accelerate the nuclear arms race rather than protect America. The system is intended to serve as a defensive measure.

title: NASA-Funded Research May Create Chinese Security Risk, Report Says
summary: A Congressional report suggests NASA-funded research since 2015 may involve Chinese researchers, posing potential security risks. The findings highlight concerns over collaborations with China in space-related scientific projects.

title: From Design Reviews to Decision Intelligence: CoLab Rewires Engineering Workflows
summary: CoLab is transforming engineering workflows by improving decision-making processes in design reviews. This shift aims to enhance efficiency and reduce complexity in engineering production.

title: CSF Forecasts Launch Supply Running Low
summary: The Commercial Spaceflight Federation (CSF) forecasts potential launch supply shortages depending on the scenario, with annual satellite launches ranging from 6,000 to 230,000, requiring up to 7,000 launches per year.

title: Vast Launches Satellite Bus Business Line
summary: Vast, a commercial space station company, announced the launch of a satellite bus business line to sell low-cost, high-volume, high-power satellite buses for various missions including communications, Earth observation, and national security programs.

title: York Space Systems to Acquire Solestial
summary: York Space Systems is acquiring Solestial to expand its capabilities in space technology. This acquisition marks a significant move in York Space Systems' merger and acquisition activities this year.

title: Astrolab Unveils Payloads Flying on FLIP Lunar Mission
summary: Astrolab has announced the payloads that will fly on its upcoming FLIP lunar mission, set to launch this year. The mission will carry a variety of scientific payloads to the Moon aboard a rover.

title: Potomac Database Systems Unveils Plans to Amass Lunar Data
summary: Potomac Database Systems has announced plans to collect and manage lunar data, highlighting commercial opportunities beyond traditional mining and infrastructure on the Moon.

title: U-Space Selects Skynopy to Support Multiple Missions
summary: U-Space has chosen Skynopy to support multiple missions using its ground-station-as-a-service model. Skynopy's innovative approach is rapidly gaining adoption in the space industry.

title: Intuitive Machines Bets on Lunar Comms with Goonhilly Buy
summary: Intuitive Machines acquired UK-based Goonhilly Earth Station and its US subsidiary COMSAT to bolster its lunar communications capabilities. The deal aims to provide integrated and resilient deep-space communication solutions for customers.

title: Sandhoo Named SDA Director, PAE for Missile Warning and Tracking
summary: Gurpartap 'GP' Sandhoo was named director of the Space Development Agency (SDA) effective May 11, succeeding his acting role since September. The SDA also announced a program for missile warning and tracking.

title: Northrop Grumman’s First MRV Readies for Summer Launch to Expand the Space Servicing Toolkit
summary: Northrop Grumman's SpaceLogistics is preparing to launch its first Mission Robotic Vehicle (MRV) this summer for on-orbit refueling and satellite life extension. The vehicle aims to expand the company's space servicing capabilities.

title: Eutelsat Network Solutions Adds Get SAT Aero Blade Terminal to Lineup
summary: Eutelsat Network Solutions has expanded its lineup by adding the Get SAT Aero Blade terminal, offering multi-orbit options for government and defense customers. This addition aims to enhance connectivity and service capabilities for U.S. government clients.

title: Sky Perfect JSAT Invests in Astroscale to Partner on On-Orbit Servicing
summary: Sky Perfect JSAT has invested in Astroscale to collaborate on on-orbit servicing initiatives. The partnership aims to advance space sustainability and debris management technologies.

title: Breaking the Power Constraint in Space With Star Catcher’s Andrew Rush
summary: The article discusses the challenges of power constraints in space engineering, highlighting how new technologies have improved size and weight options but power remains a critical limitation. It features insights from Star Catcher's Andrew Rush on overcoming these power constraints.

title: Commercial Space Station Builder Vast Expands Into Satellite Buses
summary: Vast, a company developing commercial space stations, is expanding into the satellite bus market with high-power satellite buses. The company announced this diversification move on Tuesday.

title: Lynk and Anterix Target D2D for Critical Infrastructure In New Testing
summary: Lynk Global and Anterix are collaborating to test direct-to-device (D2D) communications for critical infrastructure sectors using private networks. The partnership aims to enable new use cases for secure and reliable connectivity.

title: Skynopy to Support U-Space Missions With Ground Station Services
summary: Skynopy has secured a contract with U-Space to provide ground station services for two of its small satellites. The agreement supports U-Space's operations in Toulouse.

title: Space Force Awards Northrop Grumman $398 Million For Enhanced PTS-P
summary: The U.S. Space Force awarded Northrop Grumman a $398 million contract to develop and build an Enhanced Protected Tactical Satellite Communications-Prototype (PTS-P). This contract falls under the Space Systems Command (SSC) to advance satellite communications technology.

title: iDirectGov Releases WCore Virtualization Tool, Supporting 16 Waveforms
summary: iDirect Government released WCore, a new virtualization tool supporting 16 waveforms for rapid deployment, scaling, and management. The tool aims to enhance efficiency in waveform operations.
